detection_signals: Port makers add brandy at any point they feel like
Fortification doesn't really affect the sweetness
All Port wines are fortified the same way
why_incorrect: Fortification timing is deliberate and precise, not random. In Port production, grape brandy is added at a specific point to stop fermentation and retain residual sugar.
misconception: Port is fortified by adding brandy at a random point during fermentation to stop it and create sweetness.
corrected_understanding: Port is fortified at a planned point during fermentation with a calculated amount of neutral spirit. Timing is deliberate and affects final characteristics.
